title: AUC-safe OOF calibration and specialist blending with sigmoid, not isotonic
summary: You care about ROC AUC, so preserving ranking matters.
rationale: You care about ROC AUC, so preserving ranking matters. Scikit-learn notes isotonic can change AUC by introducing ties, while sigmoid calibration is strictly monotonic. LightGBM also warns imbalance handling can hurt probability quality, which matters when blending heterogeneous specialists.
implementation_hint: Train 2-4 diverse specialists such as baseline GBDT, AFT survival score, and possibly a ranker or CatBoost categorical model. Produce strict OOF predictions, then fit a simple sigmoid/Platt calibrator or logistic stacker globally or by broad regime (`wet/dry`, `early/mid/late`, `slick/wet`). Avoid isotonic unless used only for Brier-style diagnostics.
expected_effect: Cleaner public-score transfer from diverse models without sacrificing the base ranking signal that already gives strong CV.
risk: If specialists are too correlated, calibration alone will do little. Regime-wise calibrators can overfit if bins are too small.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
